promo_headline: Alignment research, two receipts, one Vercel ship.

digest.title: A working Tuesday — papers, receipts, ship notes.
digest.body: Tuesday is the most productive day of the week and the feed reflects that. An alignment follow-up from Anthropic, a quiet ChatGPT memory upgrade, Vercel AI SDK 6, and a Corey Haines positioning teardown all show up before lunch. The implicit instruction is: pick one, ship the consequence.

items.headline: Anthropic: a follow-up to constitutional AI
items.deck: Anthropic posted a follow-up paper extending constitutional AI with model-written critique chains. This matters because the critique-chain mechanism is what governance teams will point to when arguing about audits.

items.headline: ChatGPT memory adds team workspaces
items.deck: OpenAI added team workspaces with shared memory and per-member redaction. This matters because shared memory turns ChatGPT from a personal tool into a small-team knowledge surface.

items.headline: Vercel AI SDK 6 lands
items.deck: Vercel released AI SDK 6 with native MCP support and streamlined tool calls. This matters because the SDK update collapses 200 lines of glue code per agent and shifts where the bug surface lives.

items.headline: Corey Haines: positioning teardown for a real SaaS
items.deck: Corey Haines tore down the positioning of an actual mid-stage SaaS, line by line. This matters because positioning posts that name a real company punch harder than abstract frameworks.

items.headline: Latent Space ep 201: long-context agents
items.deck: Latent Space published episode 201 on agents that hold 1M+ token context across days. This matters because long-context behavior changes which features can move from demo to default.

items.headline: Upgrade one tiny script to Vercel AI SDK 6.
items.why: Day-one migrations are cheap; week-three migrations get tangled in unrelated refactors.
items.try: Set a 30-min timer. Pick the smallest agent you have. Bump the SDK. Note the diff.

items.headline: Apply the Corey positioning teardown to your own page.
items.why: The frame is fresh enough that the exercise still feels like a checklist, not theater.
items.try: Set a 45-min timer. Open your homepage. Run the same line-by-line teardown. Mark three lines to rewrite.
